pattern: Count pairs whose hour-values are congruent modulo 24 to complementary remainders — reduce each value to its remainder mod 24 and use a frequency map for O(n) counting.
hints: Two hours h1 and h2 form a complete day if (h1 + h2) is divisible by 24. Reduce each to h % 24 and ask: which pairs of remainders sum to 0 or 24?
Group values by their remainder mod 24. For remainder r != 0 and r != 12, pair count = freq[r] * freq[24-r]; handle r=0 and r=12 as C(freq,2) within the same group.
With n <= 100 you can also use a brute-force O(n^2) double loop — the fast approach still avoids mistakes on boundary remainders.

edge_inputs.description: example 2: multiples of 24 pair among themselves
all elements are 12 (remainder 12, pairs sum to 24)
large hour values that reduce to complementary remainders

stress.description: random hours array length up to 100 with values up to 1e9
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, size)
    hours = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [hours]
